title: Retro-orbital injection of virus or dye in mice

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"i4Yva","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"4uOVE","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"PfzAH","text":"STEP MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"DD8QY","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"5ua5b","text":"It is important that the needel used has low friction so that the injection is at a slow and consisitnat rate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204769,"guid":"41ADED31341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe","mol_weight":0,"sku":"09230","url":"https://www.adwdiabetes.com/product/5741/ulticare-u100-pet-syringes-29g-0-3cc-12mm-29g","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":783,"name":"Ulticare","link":"http://www.ulticare.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204769,"guid":"41ADED31341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe","mol_weight":0,"sku":"09230","url":"https://www.adwdiabetes.com/product/5741/ulticare-u100-pet-syringes-29g-0-3cc-12mm-29g","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":783,"name":"Ulticare","link":"http://www.ulticare.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Prepare either virus dilution (5.0e11-1.0e12) with PBS prepare or a dye aliquot . Injection is up to 200ul per mouse.
Take one mouse from the cage into an induction chamber and start isoflurane at 3% with flow rate of ~1L/min
After the mouse has slow breathing (~1/s) move the mouse to a nose cone delivery of isoflurane at 1.5% with around the same flow rate.
Expose the eye ball with two fingers (above and below) and make sure the you are not pressing on the trachea and the mouse's breathing is regular.

Use a 29G syringe at a 30-degree angle beveled downwards until you hit the bone, retract a bit and slowly inject. See cited paper for more details. Retro-orbital injections in mice. LabAnimal. 29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe Ulticare Catalog #09230
Remove the needle slowly and press the eyelid shut for a few seconds.

Validating the injection was successful is possible with JF525 by looking at the pee of the animal after it wakes up. Leaving the animal in a clean cup with kim-wips will collect the pee and it would seem pink.
creator: Boaz Mohar
